game: Arnhem - Operation Market-Garden, September 1944 (SPI Westwall quad, 1976)

sources.standard_rules: Westwall Standard Rules folder (SPI 1976) - OCR sidecar C:/VassalLibrary/_meta/ocr/'Westwall_ Four Battles to Germany__WestWallStdRules.txt' (32.4k chars, complete 1.0-9.14), scanned PDF in C:/VassalLibrary/launchbox/Manuals/VASSAL/'Westwall_ Four Battles to Germany'/
sources.exclusive_rules: Arnhem Exclusive Rules (same folder, OCR sidecar __WestWallQuad.txt chars 33452-63962, complete 10.0-18.26 + design credits + printed CRT)
sources.crt_source_2: C:/VassalIngest/westwall/map_tables_transcription.json - mastermind's independent image-only transcription of the printed CRT + Terrain Key (2026-07-09)
sources.counters: C:/VassalIngest/westwall/counter_stats_by_image.json (mastermind factor->image binding) + full 95-counter contact-sheet visual pass 2026-07-11 (The Vassal) - every front-state counter read by eye; 18 fired-state artillery images are flip sides
sources.module: Westwall Quad.vmod, extracted at C:/VassalIngest/westwall/extracted/ - board 'Arnhem' img 'Base Layer(1).png', HexGrid dx=96 dy=119 x0=60 y0=60 (= hand-validated legacy values), setup 'Arnhem Historical.vsav' (save key 0xA3)

crt.cite: [7.61] Integrated Combat Results Table (printed in the Exclusive Rules folder; identical for all four Westwall battles)
crt.type: differential, terrain-integrated: differential = total attacking strength (attack + barrage + GSP) minus total defending strength (defense + FPF); the DEFENDER's terrain row maps the differential to a result column; die 1-6 x column -> result

results.Ae: Attacker Eliminated - all attacking units eliminated; defender may advance [7.62]
results.Br: Both retreat one hex, DEFENDER FIRST (a surrounded defender is eliminated) [7.62]
results.De: Defender Eliminated - all defending units eliminated; attacker may advance [7.62]

crt.bounds: attacks below the lowest differential resolve at the lowest; above +12 resolve as +12 (printed CRT notes)
crt.worked_example: [7.0] PROCEDURE example: 13 points attacking 4 points in a Town hex = +9, resolved at +9-11 on the Town line; die 5 -> D1
crt.two_source_note: OCR text of the printed table and the mastermind image transcription agree on all 12x6 result cells, all 4 terrain rows' brackets, both bounds notes, and the diagonal one-column-per-die-row shift; also identical to engine/rules.py (validated 2026-07-03)

terrain_effects_chart.cite: Terrain Key printed on the mapsheet (mastermind transcription; matches Standard Rules 5.2 cases)

hexside.canal: PROHIBITED (unless bridge) - demolished canal/rail bridge hexsides are 'treated as a normal Canal or River hexside' [12.13], i.e. prohibited
hexside.ferry: +3 MP; ferries are NON-BRIDGE hexsides: no ZOC or attacks across them [Terrain Key; 6.33]
hexside.bridge_rail: no additional MP; demolishable [12.11-12.15], never repairable [12.16]
hexside.bridge_canal: no additional MP; demolishable, repairable by the Allied Engineer only [12.16/13.1]
hexside.road: 1/2 MP road hex to adjacent road hex through a road hexside, regardless of hex terrain [5.22]
hexside.trail: 1 MP trail hex to adjacent trail hex through a trail hexside, regardless of hex terrain [5.23]

movement.sequence: [4.1] GT = First Player-Turn (Movement Phase then Combat Phase) then Second Player-Turn then GT marker advance; Allied player is First Player [18.17]
movement.basic: [5.0/5.21] 1 MP per clear hex, terrain per TEC, hexside costs are IN ADDITION to hex costs, costs cumulative [5.21]; may not enter without sufficient MP for hex + hexside [5.25]; MA never exceeded, no accumulation/transfer [5.13]
movement.enemy_hex: [5.12] never enter a hex containing an enemy unit
movement.zoc_exit: [5.14/6.13] a unit may NEVER exit an enemy-controlled hex during any Movement Phase - only retreat/advance as a combat result, or removal of the exerting unit
movement.vehicle_classes: [5.24] armored, reconnaissance, mechanized and self-propelled artillery units may never enter a rough, broken or forest (woods) hex, or pass through a river or stream hexside, EXCEPT through road or trail hexsides; if forced to retreat in violation they are eliminated instead
movement.stacking: [5.31] a friendly unit may move THROUGH friendly hexes at no cost but may not END the Movement or Combat Phase stacked - STACKING IS PROHIBITED (sole exceptions: one airborne/glider unit with the Engineer 13.24; DZ counters stack freely 15.35)
movement.supply: [5.16] there are no supply rules; isolation has no effect
movement.zoc: [6.0] six surrounding hexes; all units must cease movement on entering an enemy-controlled hex; [6.11] exerted at all times by all units; [6.12] no extra MP to enter; [6.31] into all terrain except hexsides prohibited to all unit types; [6.32] vehicle-class ZOCs DO extend into broken/rough/woods and those units may attack into them; [6.33] ZOC never extends through non-bridge river hexsides (ferries are non-bridge); ZOC does extend across non-bridge stream hexsides
movement.city_is_town: [11.0] City hexes are treated exactly like Town hexes except the 11.1 retreat-reduction benefit

combat.mandatory: [7.0] combat between adjacent opposing units is mandatory; [7.11] every non-phasing unit with a phasing unit adjacent must be attacked; [7.12] all phasing units ending movement in an EZOC must attack, and all adjacent friendly units participate; [7.21] a phasing unit in the ZOC of more than one enemy must attack all those not engaged by some other attacking unit; [7.14] each unit attacks once and is attacked once per Combat Phase; [7.15] non-artillery only attack adjacent; [6.33] non-artillery may never attack across non-bridge river hexsides
combat.multi_hex: [7.22/7.23] units in different hexes may combine against a single hex if all attackers are adjacent to (or in range of) it; single combat vs multiple defenders requires ALL attacking units adjacent to ALL defending units (barraging artillery exempt)
combat.unity: [7.3] attack/defense strengths are unitary - never divided among combats
combat.terrain: [7.41] defender only benefits from terrain; [7.42] stream hexside benefit only if ALL attackers attack across stream hexsides; [7.44] most advantageous terrain applies; [7.45] multiple defenders on different terrain lines - all benefit from the line most favorable to the defender
combat.diversionary: [7.51] poor-differential holding attacks legal; [7.52] the differential of an attack may NEVER be voluntarily reduced
combat.retreats: [7.7] owning player moves his units the indicated number of hexes away from the combat position, immediately; eliminated if unable; [7.71] first hex may leave an EZOC, thereafter never enter one; friendly units do NOT negate EZOC for retreats; [7.72] never across prohibited hexsides (incl. 5.24 class prohibitions); [7.73] through vacant hexes where possible, displacing friends only if no other route; [7.74] must end the full indicated distance away - a partial retreat eliminates the unit in the last hex reached
combat.displacement: [7.81] displaced friend moves one hex as if retreating, chains allowed [7.82]; if displacement would eliminate the displaced unit the RETREATING unit is eliminated instead; displaced artillery that has not yet fired may not fire this phase; a hex the phasing player intended to barrage may not be attacked after a retreating unit displaces into it [7.82-second]
combat.advance: [7.91] retreating/eliminated enemies leave a Path of Retreat; ANY OR ALL victorious adjacent participating units may advance along it; [7.92] may stop anywhere along it; [7.93] ignore EZOC; [7.94] non-adjacent artillery never advances; [7.95] may not stray from the path; [7.96] exercised immediately, before any other combat resolution, never forced; advanced units may neither attack nor be attacked that phase; [7.97] example: a 3-hex retreat may be followed by up to three victorious units
combat.city: [11.1] units occupying/entering/retreating into City hexes reduce all remaining retreat results by TWO hexes: D1/D2/Br/A1/A2 may become no-effect at the owner's option; D3 retreats at least 1, D4 at least 2; eliminations unaffected; airborne artillery receives NO reduction; [11.2] when surrounded (all six adjacent hexes enemy-occupied or enemy-controlled) only Allied airborne and glider units keep the benefit; attacks on city hexes use the Town CRT line

artillery.factors: [2.31/2.32] artillery: Barrage Strength - FPF Strength - Range Allowance / Defense Strength - Movement Allowance; range excludes firer's hex, includes target's [8.12/2.32]
artillery.barrage: [8.11] may attack non-adjacent enemies within range, never forced; NO Line of Sight restrictions [8.61]; [8.13] barraging attacks a single hex except in combined attacks; [8.14] barraging artillery suffers no combat results; [8.15] attacks solely by artillery and/or GSP: only D2, D3, D4, De affect the defender
artillery.combined: [8.21/8.22] barrage may join adjacent attacks (combined attack); needs range to only one of the defending hexes
artillery.adjacent: [8.31] artillery adjacent to an enemy MUST participate in an attack using its Barrage Strength; [8.33] suffers all combat results when adjacent; [8.34] artillery adjacent only across river hexsides is not forced, may barrage others, and suffers no results when attacking across a river hexside
artillery.fpf: [8.41] non-phasing artillery may add FPF to ONE friendly defending unit if: not successfully attacked this Combat Phase, no adverse result in the previous Combat Phase, not displaced, not adjacent to an enemy (except across a river); [8.42] range counted to the FRIENDLY unit's hex; [8.43] FPF adds to the defense strength; [8.44] the artillery neither benefits nor suffers from the result; [8.45] no FPF for a defender attacked solely by artillery/GSP; [8.46] each artillery unit uses FPF once per GAME-TURN; [8.0] a unit may use Barrage AND FPF in the same Game-Turn
artillery.defense: [8.5] attacked artillery defends with its Defense Strength
artillery.terrain: [8.62] defenders get full HEX terrain benefit vs artillery; hexside benefits only against combined attacks whose non-artillery components all attack across the hexside type
artillery.arnhem_restrictions: [14.11] only the Allied player receives GSP, usable as Barrage or FPF only within THREE hexes of an Allied NON-AIRBORNE unit; [14.12] no more than TWO artillery units per single combat for either barrage or FPF (no limit on GSP)

ground_support: [9.0] GSP allotted per Game-Turn by the scenario; function exactly as artillery (barrage when phasing, FPF when not); [9.11] 1 GSP = 1 strength point; [9.12] split freely between targets; [9.13] unlimited range (14.11 overrides for Arnhem: within 3 hexes of a non-airborne Allied unit); [9.14] never accumulated - unused points are lost

bridge_demolition.trigger: at the instant the FIRST Allied unit moves into a hex of which a Canal or Railroad Bridge forms a side, the German player MAY (his option, never forced) attempt demolition - immediately, no matter what the phase [12.11/12.12]
bridge_demolition.roll: die 1-2 demolishes [12.12]; failure = the bridge is intact for the REST OF THE GAME, no further attempt ever [12.14]
bridge_demolition.effect: demolished bridge hexside becomes a normal Canal or River hexside immediately [12.13]
bridge_demolition.scope: only Canal and Railroad bridges; Highway bridges over rivers NEVER demolishable [12.15]; all bridges' movement effects identical [12.15]
bridge_demolition.repair: only Canal bridges, only by the Allied Engineer [12.16]; railroad bridges never repaired
bridge_demolition.bookkeeping: [12.17] players record demolished bridges - the engine state carries this

engineer.repair: [13.1] Engineer stationary adjacent to a demolished Canal Bridge hexside, free of EZOC, for one entire GERMAN player-turn -> repaired for the rest of the game; may repair as many bridges as it is adjacent to in that turn
engineer.crossing: [13.21] Engineer adjacent to a River hexside, expending no MP that Allied player-turn and free of EZOC, lets AIRBORNE AND GLIDER units (not airborne artillery [13.23]) cross the river through its hex in that or subsequent Movement Phases; [13.22] crossing unit passes through the Engineer's hex to the hex across the river (or vice versa), paying terrain cost, no river hexside charge; [13.23] may cross into an enemy-controlled hex
engineer.assault: [13.24] ONE airborne/glider unit may end the Movement Phase stacked with the Engineer and MUST attack one adjacent enemy across an adjacent river hexside, resolved on the STREAM line; if unable to advance across the river after combat it is eliminated (special exception to no-stacking and no-attack-across-river)
engineer.retreat_bar: [13.25] Allied units never retreat after combat through ANY river hexside or out of the Engineer's hex (eliminated instead); German units never advance after combat across a river hexside
engineer.replacement: [13.3] eliminated Engineer re-enters on the following Allied Movement Phase at 0105 or 0106; the destroyed unit counts for German VP

reinforcements.ground_entry: [15.11-15.13] entry hex costs per Terrain Key; road-entry columns pay road rate for hypothetical off-map road hexes: lead unit 1/2 MP, second 1, third 1.5, ... (worked example 15.13); [15.14] may arrive any time during the Movement Phase, then move normally
reinforcements.blocked: [15.21] never enter an enemy-occupied hex; may enter an EZOC but stop there; [15.22] if and only if the scheduled hex is enemy-occupied or friendly-occupied-in-EZOC, enter at the nearest unblocked mapedge hex toward the nearest friendly unit; [15.23/15.24] may be withheld to a later turn but must use the scheduled hex or 15.22 alternate; [15.25] off-map reinforcements have no effect on play
reinforcements.airborne: [15.31] listed as arriving within ONE hex of a specified hex, allied player's choice, ONE UNIT PER HEX; may arrive in an EZOC; arrival in an enemy-occupied hex = eliminated; [15.32] MA is 3 on the arrival turn (considered to have expended 4 MP); [15.33] may not land on ANY occupied hex (friendly or enemy) - eliminated if forced; [15.34] delay only under the (optional) Weather rules
reinforcements.dz_counters: [15.35] DZ counters are not units: no ZOC, no strength, both sides move through/stack freely, never destroyed, may be overrun; they anchor airborne LOC [17.32]; [15.36] Historical Scenario: placed on the specified hexes
reinforcements.german_exit: [15.4] only the German player exits; pays the exit-hex-terrain-equivalent MP for the imaginary off-map hex; [15.41] west edge between 0601 and 2301 inclusive, east edge between 0126 and 2726 inclusive; [15.42] exited units return as reinforcements on any subsequent GT on the SAME edge segment they left by; [15.43] exit only in the German Movement Phase; exit by combat result = eliminated

weather: [16.0] OPTIONAL in the Historical Scenario (18.17: only if both players agree) - NOT USED in this encoding; the base Historical Scenario uses the fixed 18.13 arrival hexes and the fixed 18.16 GSP allotment

victory.allied_vp: [17.11] 1 VP per German unit eliminated; 5 VP per non-airborne non-glider unit north of the Waal (2726-3005) at the END OF EACH GT with a LOC; 10 VP per non-airborne non-glider unit north of the Neder Rijn (3706-3424) at the END OF THE GAME with a LOC off the map
victory.german_vp: [17.12] 5 VP per Allied unit destroyed; 3 VP at the end of each GERMAN player-turn per Allied unit unable to trace a LOC [17.35]
victory.loc_ground: [17.31] non-airborne Allied units trace contiguous hexes off the south edge at 0105/0106; once the line enters a trail hex all remaining hexes connect by road or trail hexsides; once it enters a road hex, by road hexsides only
victory.loc_airborne: [17.32] airborne units (all that entered as airborne reinforcements, except Polish) trace max SEVEN hexes to the DZ counter matching their DIVISIONAL designation, any terrain
victory.loc_common: [17.33] never into/through enemy units or EZOC; friendly units DO negate EZOC for LOC; [17.34] never through unbridged river or stream hexsides
victory.exempt: [17.36] Polish and all German units are never subject to LOC
victory.levels: [17.4] ratio German VP : Allied VP - >=3.0 German Strategic; 2.01-2.99 German Tactical; 2.0 Draw; 1.0-1.99 Allied Tactical; <=1.0 Allied Strategic

scenario_historical.cite: [18.1] Scenario I: Historical Scenario; Allied is First Player, 10 Game-Turns [18.17]

german_initial.note: OCR printed '2/98' - counter art reads 2/9SS 4-4-7 mech

allied_airborne.cite: [18.13] placed within one hex of the listed hex, one unit per hex [15.31]

german_reinforcements.cite: [18.15] enter on or between the listed mapedge hexes

gt1.note: schedule prints '1/10S' on the artillery line

gt2.note: schedule: '5-3-10 Hex: 2107 (0726)' - the armor variant, counter-verified
schedule: '4-4-10 Hex: 2107 (0726)' - the mech variant, counter-verified

gt3.note: schedule prints '9S' on the artillery line

module_deviations_worksheet: The module's 'Arnhem Historical' setup PRE-PLACES the GT1 airborne reinforcements STACKED on their 18.13 target hexes (e.g. 1/502+2/502+3/502 all in 1004) - the printed rules drop them as GT1 reinforcements, player-placed within one hex of the target, ONE PER HEX [15.31], and stacking is prohibited [5.31]. Our scenario holds them as GT1 airborne reinforcements per the rules.
The module setup also pre-places 1/82 at 2223 (stacked with the 508 battalions) and 1/1Lt at 3718 - same deviation, same handling.
The module setup parks all later-turn reinforcements in map-margin holding boxes (rows 00-02, cols 30-37); our scenario carries them in the reserve pool with due turns instead.

Piece display names collide ('A 1', 'A 2' appear on many counters) - image filenames are the unique keys; German pieces have NO side prefix distinct from Allied ('A ' = Arnhem battle prefix, both sides).

binding_corrections.note: counter_stats_by_image.json entries corrected after the 2026-07-11 contact-sheet pass (all 95 front images read by eye; mastermind's ambiguous_bind flags honored per the 2026-07-09 brief)
